greetingDefault: You come bearing a letter of introduction, I understand. I have seconds. Speak.
greetingFriendly: You have proven your worth to this house. Sit. Tell me what you've found.
greetingHostile: The audacity of approaching me directly without appointment is... impressive. Briefly.
farewellDefault: You are dismissed. Return when you have something worth reporting.
unknownKeywordResponse: That is not a matter I address with strangers.

keywords.response: Baron Alexei of House Juandaastas — though the title grows heavier each year. Planetary Governor of Aragon by appointment of the Emperor himself, though the Emperor's appointments have grown somewhat... theoretical of late.

I govern. I arbitrate disputes between the merchant guilds and the Church. I manage the taxation that funds a garrison that is too small for the unrest I anticipate. And I attend an endless sequence of dinners that settle nothing.
House Juandaastas was granted Aragon three generations ago. We are not the most powerful house in the Known Worlds — but we are here. The other great houses have larger problems to manage. For now.

Serfs have been disappearing from the outlying farms. A preacher in the lower quarter claims the suns' fading is the Baron's fault — specifically mine. And someone is distributing pamphlets calling for a 'league of the common-born.' I take these things seriously, which is more than my predecessors can say.
Seven in the past month. The wilderness party I sent found — nothing. No bodies, no tracks. The Inquisitor thinks it is Antinomist. I think it is something older.

The suns dim. Every astronomer agrees on the data. None agree on the cause. The Church calls it prophecy. The League calls it a market opportunity. I call it something that will force my hand before I am ready.

Emperor Alexius presses for reunification of the noble houses. A noble cause. But here on Aragon, that means my house is asked to bleed for a cause that benefits houses closer to the center. I have written. My letters are politely acknowledged.
Lady Seraphina handles the Palace's interior matters with rather more competence than I handle its exterior ones. She has been... quiet lately. Spending time in the library. I attribute it to stress.

If you are truly here to be useful rather than merely ambitious, I have a task. The wilderness disappearances — I need a discreet investigation. Not Harkan's blunt approach. Something quieter. Can you do quiet?
You are dismissed. Return when you have something worth reporting.
